summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

If theoretical yield is 20 g and actual yield is 18 g, what is the percent yield?

(18/20)×100 = 90%

If theoretical yield = 40 g and percent yield = 65%, what is actual yield?

Actual yield = 0.65 × 40 g = 26 g.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Limiting reagent determines theoretical yield because:

The limiting reagent is completely consumed first; once gone, no more product can form — it defines the maximum (theoretical) yield.
